name: Logis de la Cadene
summary: A Michelin-starred Saint-Emilion destination meal on a small medieval square, best for a planned wine-country lunch or dinner.
whyItMatters: If Saint-Emilion gets one high-memory meal, this has the strongest official case: oldest restaurant in town, Angelus-family ownership context, and a serious wine-country setting.
pros: Official site has a table reservation link
Official site states it was founded in 1848 and is the oldest restaurant in Saint-Emilion
Michelin-starred chef and substantial wine focus are documented by the restaurant
cons: Not useful for quick food or race-day logistics
Official page did not expose current opening hours in browsable text

logistics.opening_hours: Unknown from official page text; verify on booking page.
logistics.reservation_needed: Yes, essential for a destination meal.
logistics.distance_from_base: Unknown until Saint-Emilion/Bordeaux lodging and day plan are confirmed.
logistics.distance_from_viewing_point: Not a race-day viewing food option.
logistics.transit: Saint-Emilion village access; verify train/taxi or parking plan from Bordeaux.
logistics.parking: Unknown; medieval village parking/walking constraints likely.
logistics.road_closure_notes: No Tour de France closure relevance found for this non-viewing day candidate.

timing.best_visit_window: Saint-Emilion lunch or dinner on a wine-country day, booked well ahead.
timing.race_day_risk: Low if scheduled away from race-day transfers; high if squeezed after Stage 7.

sources.title: Logis de la Cadene official website
sources.note: Supports address, reservation link, restaurant history, Saint-Emilion setting, Michelin-starred chef, and wine-country positioning.

open_questions: Verify July 2026 opening hours and table availability.
Confirm whether the group wants a formal starred meal during the Saint-Emilion portion.
